ogTitle: Cradoc vs Cramps Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cradoc and Cramps Bay.
heading: Cradoc vs Cramps Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cradoc, Tasmania and Cramps Bay, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cradoc has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Cramps Bay (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cradoc or Cramps Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cradoc has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Cramps Bay scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cradoc scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Cramps Bay scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cradoc or Cramps Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cradoc has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
